app.dsa-solver.page.givenAnArrayOfIntege:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
app.dsa-solver.page.givenTheHeadOfASingl: Given the `head` of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
app.dsa-solver.page.givenAStringSContain:
Given a string `s` containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

An input string is valid if open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets and closed in the correct order.
app.dsa-solver.page.compilingAndRunningA: Compiling and running against test cases...

app.interview-prep.page.whatIsAClosureIn: What is a closure in JavaScript?
app.interview-prep.page.aClosureIsTheCom: A closure is the combination of a function bundled together (enclosed) with references to its surrounding state (the lexical environment). In other words, a closure gives an inner function access to the outer function's scope even after the outer function has returned.
app.interview-prep.page.explainTheDiffer: Explain the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases.
app.interview-prep.page.sqlDatabasesAreR: SQL databases are relational, table-based, have predefined schema, and scale vertically (buying bigger servers). NoSQL databases are non-relational, document/key-value/graph-based, have dynamic schemas, and scale horizontally (adding more servers).
app.interview-prep.page.howDoYouDetectAC: How do you detect a cycle in a linked list?
app.interview-prep.page.youCanDetectACyc: You can detect a cycle using Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm (also known as the Tortoise and the Hare algorithm). You use two pointers: a slow pointer that moves 1 step at a time, and a fast pointer that moves 2 steps at a time. If there is a cycle, the fast pointer will eventually meet the slow pointer. If they hit null/end, there is no cycle.
app.interview-prep.page.howDoYouHandleCo: How do you handle conflict with a co-worker or teammate?
app.interview-prep.page.usingTheStarMeth: Using the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result): I stay calm, listen actively to their perspective without interrupting, focus on the objective/problem rather than personal feelings, identify a compromise or shared goal, and check in later to ensure a positive working relationship. For example, when a teammate and I disagreed on code architecture, we listed the pros and cons of both, consulted senior guidelines, and chose the one that met performance requirements better.
app.interview-prep.page.whatAreReactHook: What are React hooks, and why were they introduced?
app.interview-prep.page.hooksAreFunction: Hooks are functions that let you 'hook into' React state and lifecycle features from function components. They were introduced in React 16.8 to resolve class component issues: reusing stateful logic without changing component hierarchy, avoiding complex lifecycle methods, and keeping related logic grouped together.

app.page.throughEducationCommunityEn: Through education, community engagement, and targeted programs, we're building a future where opportunity knows no boundaries. Every number represents a real story of change.
app.page.nextgenDevbhoomiFoundationI: Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation is a dedicated non-profit organization focused on bridging the digital divide. We believe that modern technical education and resources should be accessible to everyone, regardless of their background or geographic location.
app.page.byOfferingIndustryorientedI: By offering industry-oriented IT internships, mentorship, and community programs, we strive to build a resilient, self-reliant future for students and aspiring professionals across the region.

app.page.theNextgenDevbhoomiProgram: The Nextgen Devbhoomi program was the turning point of my college life. The instructors provided deep practical insights that are not taught in college classes. The DSA solver helped me crack product interviews.
app.page.comingFromATier3CollegeIn: Coming from a tier-3 college in Uttarakhand, I lacked confidence and resources. Through the Devbhoomi scholarship program, I learned React, web architecture, and got mentored directly by industry experts. I landed my dream job!
app.page.theLibraryAndCodingBootcam: The library and coding bootcamps are fully community-oriented. Working on open-source projects under a personal mentor gave me hands-on confidence. Highly recommend this foundation to all developers.
app.page.ourGraduatesHaveGoneFromL: Our graduates have gone from learning basics in small villages to building production software at top global companies. Hear directly about their journeys.
app.page.rateAchievedWithin6Months: Rate achieved within 6 months of program graduation.
app.page.areDonationsToTheNextgenD: Are donations to the Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation tax-exempt?
app.page.yesNextgenDevbhoomiFoundati: Yes, Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation is a registered NGO under the Societies Registration Act and holds 80G tax-exemption approval. All contributions qualify for a 50% tax deduction under Section 80G of the Income Tax Act.
app.page.whoIsEligibleToApplyForT: Who is eligible to apply for the IT Internship Program?
app.page.theProgramIsOpenToUndergr: The program is open to undergraduate and postgraduate technical students (B.Tech, BCA, MCA, BSc IT) as well as self-taught developers. We give special placement priority to students belonging to regional communities in the Himalayan foothills.
app.page.howDoesTheDigitalLibraryS: How does the Digital Library support student learning?
app.page.theDigitalLibraryProvidesF: The Digital Library provides free access to curated engineering books, curriculum guides, interview reference sheets, and standard test prep materials. It is designed to save students from purchasing expensive academic resources.
app.page.canIVolunteerRemotelyOrPa: Can I volunteer remotely or part-time?
app.page.absolutelyWeOfferFlexibleR: Absolutely! We offer flexible remote mentoring roles where developers guide students over online portals. Local event support is also available for regional workshops and campus ambassador activities.
